title: Pulsed dc motor drive circuit
abstract: A drive circuit (1) comprises first and second circuit loops (7, 8) connected in parallel across a DC power supply (20) to deliver pulsed DC to the power connection arrangement (31) of an electric motor (30) which is arranged to provide a fluctuating impedance in the first circuit loop (7). The first circuit loop (7) includes a first inductor (2) arranged in series with the first winding (4) of a transformer (6), while the second circuit loop (8) includes a second inductor (3) arranged in series with the second winding (5) of the transformer (6). The first and second windings (4, 5) of the transformer (6) are inductively coupled such that, if the first winding (4) were connected across a power source (40) and the second winding (5) were connected across a load (41), then a rise in current flowing into a first end (4') of the first winding (4) would induce a voltage in the second winding (5) to cause a current to flow out of a first end (5') of the second winding (5). The first ends (4', 5') of both windings are arranged between the respective winding and the positive pole (21) of the power supply. A blocking element (9) is arranged to prevent current from flowing through the second circuit loop (8) from the positive pole (21) to the negative pole (22) of the power supply (20).
claims:
Claims 1, A drive circuit (1) for supplying DC power from a DC power supply (20) to a power connection arrangement (31) of an electric motor (30), the power supply having a positive pole (21) and a negative pole (22); the power connection arrangement (31) being configured to supply power from the drive circuit to windings (34) of the motor, the power connection arrangement and windings being configured to provide a fluctuating impedance in the drive circuit in use; the drive circuit (1) comprising: a first inductor (2), a second inductor (3), a transformer (6), and a blocking element (9); the transformer (6) comprising first and second windings (4, 5), each winding having a first end (4', 5') and a second end (4", 5"); the first and second windings being inductively coupled such that, if the first winding (4) were connected across a power source (40) and the second winding (5) were connected across a load (41), then a rise in current flowing into the first end (4') of the first winding (4) would induce a voltage in the second winding (5) to cause a current to flow out of the first end (S') of the second winding (5); the first inductor (2), the first winding (4), and the power connection arrangement (31) of the motor being electrically connected in series across the positive and negative poles (21, 22) of the power supply to form a first circuit loop (7); the second inductor (3), the second winding (5), and the blocking element (9) being electrically connected in series across the positive and negative poles (21, 22) of the power supply to form a second circuit loop (8); the first end (4') of the first winding (4) being arranged in the first circuit loop (7) between the second end (4") of the first winding (4) and the positive pole (21) of the power supply; 

 the first end (S') of the second winding (5) being arranged in the second circuit loop (8) between the second end (5") of the second winding (5) and the positive pole (21) of the power supply; the blocking element (9) being arranged to permit current to flow in the second circuit loop (8) in a direction away from the negative pole (22) of the power supply and towards the positive pole (21) of the power supply, and to prevent current from flowing in the second circuit loop (8) in a direction away from the positive pole (21) of the power supply and towards the negative pole (22) of the power supply. 2. A drive circuit according to claim 1, wherein the first inductor (2) and the first winding (4) are arranged in the first circuit loop (7) between the power connection arrangement (31) of the motor and one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply. 3. A drive circuit according to claim 2, wherein the second winding (5) and the second inductor (3) are arranged in the second circuit loop (8) between said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply and the blocking element (9). 4. A drive circuit according to claim 2, wherein the second winding (5) is arranged between the second inductor (3) and said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply. 5. A drive circuit according to claim 4, wherein the second winding (5) and the second inductor (3) are arranged in the second circuit loop (8) between said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply and the blocking element (9). 6. A drive circuit according to claim 2, wherein the first inductor (2) is arranged between the first winding (4) and said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply. 7. A drive circuit according to claim 6, wherein the second winding (5) and the second inductor (3) are arranged in the second circuit loop (8) between said one respective pole (21, 22} of the power supply and the blocking element (9). 8. A drive circuit according to claim 6, wherein the second winding (5) is arranged between the second inductor (3) and said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply. 9. A drive circuit according to claim 8, wherein the second winding (5) and the second inductor (3) are arranged in the second circuit loop (8) between said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply and the blocking element (9). 10. A drive circuit according to any of claims 2 - 9, wherein said one respective pole (21, 22) of the power supply is the positive pole (21) of the power supply. 11. A drive circuit according to any preceding claim, wherein the blocking component (9) consists of a diode or a group of diodes. 12. An apparatus for use with a DC power supply (20), the power supply having a positive pole (21) and a negative pole (22), the apparatus comprising: an electric motor (30) having a power connection arrangement (31), and a drive circuit (1) according to claim 1; the power connection arrangement (31) being configured to supply power from the drive circuit (1) to windings (34) of the motor and to provide a fluctuating impedance in the drive circuit (1) in use. 13. An apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the power connection arrangement (31) comprises a brushed commutator (35). 14 An apparatus according to claim 12 and including a DC power supply (20), wherein the power supply comprises a rechargeable battery having a positive pole (21) and a negative pole (22), the power supply (20) being configured to supply DC power to the drive circuit (1). 15. A method of driving an electric motor (30) from a DC power supply (20), comprising: providing a drive circuit (1) according to claim 1, supplying DC power from the DC power supply (20) via the drive circuit (1) to a power connection arrangement (31), and supplying power from the drive circuit (1) via the power connection arrangement (31) to windings (34) of the motor, wherein the power connection arrangement (31) is configured to provide a fluctuating impedance in the first circuit loop (7) of the drive circuit (1).
